description: High-value DAX patterns distilled for teaching a BI agent. Code+key-rules depth (not exhaustive). All examples use the reference retail schema in references/retail-schema.md and are original teaching code, not reproductions.

default_source.primary: DAX Patterns, 2nd ed., Marco Russo & Alberto Ferrari (SQLBI, 2020)
default_source.supporting: The Definitive Guide to DAX, 2nd ed., Russo & Ferrari
default_source.note: Rules and structure are paraphrased; DAX shown is original teaching code on a fictional retail schema. No verbatim book code or sample model is reproduced.

patterns.purpose: Accumulate a measure from the start of the year (or quarter/month) to the last visible date.
patterns.dax_shape: IF ( MIN('Date'[DateWithSales]), CALCULATE ( [BaseMeasure], DATESYTD ( 'Date'[Date] [, fiscalYearEnd] ) ) )
patterns.key_rule: Time-intelligence TABLE functions (DATESYTD/QTD/MTD) go only in CALCULATE filter args, on a Date table that is Marked as Date Table.
when_to_use: Standard Gregorian or simple fiscal calendar
avoid_when: Non-Gregorian, 4-4-5, or custom-week calendars (use custom/week pattern)
You need the value past the last date with data without a guard
common_mistakes: Using DATESYTD inside an iterator or as a scalar
Date table not marked -> wrong totals
No future-date guard so the line flat-lines
patterns.retail_example: Sales YTD := IF ( MIN('Date'[DateWithSales]), CALCULATE ( [Sales Amount], DATESYTD ( 'Date'[Date] ) ) )
future_agent_use: generator: emit *YTD/QTD/MTD variants from any base measure
analyzer: flag TI funcs used outside CALCULATE
model review: verify Date table is marked
patterns.source: DAX Patterns ch. Standard time-related calculations

patterns.purpose: Compare current period to the same period last year and compute growth.
patterns.dax_shape: PY := CALCULATE([m], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Date'[Date]))  |  YOY% := DIVIDE([m]-[PY],[PY])
patterns.key_rule: Build comparison measures by composition (base -> PY -> YOY -> YOY%); always DIVIDE for the ratio.
avoid_when: Custom calendars where SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR misaligns weeks
Comparing non-comparable partial periods without guarding
common_mistakes: Dividing with '/' instead of DIVIDE
Not handling blank prior period
Recomputing the base measure repeatedly instead of VAR
patterns.retail_example:
Sales PY := CALCULATE([Sales Amount], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Date'[Date]))
Sales YOY % := DIVIDE([Sales Amount]-[Sales PY],[Sales PY])
future_agent_use: generator: auto-build PY/YOY/YOY% family
analyzer: flag '/' division and missing blank guards
patterns.source: DAX Patterns ch. Comparing different time periods

patterns.purpose: Sum the trailing 12 months ending at the last visible date.
patterns.dax_shape: VAR last=MAX('Date'[Date]) VAR first=EDATE(last,-12)+1 RETURN CALCULATE([m], DATESBETWEEN('Date'[Date],first,last))
patterns.key_rule: Use SCALAR date functions (EDATE) to compute boundaries, then a TABLE function (DATESBETWEEN) inside CALCULATE.
avoid_when: Week-based rolling windows on custom calendars
common_mistakes: Using DATEADD/ENDOFMONTH where scalar math is meant
Off-by-one on the start date (forgetting +1)
patterns.retail_example: Sales MAT := VAR last=MAX('Date'[Date]) VAR first=EDATE(last,-12)+1 RETURN CALCULATE([Sales Amount], DATESBETWEEN('Date'[Date],first,last))
future_agent_use: generator: parametric trailing-N-month measures
analyzer: scalar-vs-table TI function misuse
patterns.source: DAX Patterns ch. Custom time-related calculations

patterns.purpose: Accumulate a measure over a sortable axis (typically date) up to the current row.
patterns.dax_shape: VAR cutoff=MAX('Date'[Date]) RETURN CALCULATE([m], 'Date'[Date] <= cutoff [, re-apply filters to keep])
patterns.key_rule: The '<= cutoff' filter replaces the existing axis filter; re-apply any sub-filter (e.g. weekday) that must survive.
when_to_use: Cumulative over any sortable column (use Sort-By column)
avoid_when: The axis column has no meaningful sort order
common_mistakes: Forgetting REMOVEFILTERS when Date isn't marked
Dropping a slicer filter that should persist
No future-date guard
patterns.retail_example: Sales RT := VAR cutoff=MAX('Date'[Date]) VAR res=CALCULATE([Sales Amount],'Date'[Date]<=cutoff) RETURN IF(MIN('Date'[Date])<=CALCULATE(MAX('Sales'[OrderDate]),REMOVEFILTERS()),res)
future_agent_use: generator: running-total of any base measure over a chosen axis
analyzer: detect dropped filters / missing guards

patterns.purpose: Aggregate additively across most dimensions but take first/last value across time (stock, balances).
patterns.dax_shape: CALCULATE ( SUM('Snapshot'[Value]), LASTNONBLANK('Date'[Date], CALCULATE(SUM('Snapshot'[Value]))) )
patterns.key_rule: Sum across products/stores, but never sum across time; pick the last (or first) non-blank date in context.
avoid_when: Flows/transactions that ARE additive over time (use plain SUM)
common_mistakes: Showing the measure summed across months (wrong total)
Using LASTDATE blindly when some dates are blank
patterns.retail_example: Units On Hand (EOP) := CALCULATE ( SUM('Inventory'[On Hand Quantity]), LASTNONBLANK('Date'[Date], CALCULATE(SUM('Inventory'[On Hand Quantity]))) )
future_agent_use: model review: flag snapshot facts that must not be summed over time
generator: opening/closing balance pair

patterns.purpose: Assign each entity to a fixed band from a configuration table, usable as a slicer axis.
patterns.dax_shape: Calc column: lookup the config row where Min <= entityValue < Max -> return Segment
patterns.key_rule: Static band is row-fixed -> implement as a CALCULATED COLUMN so it can group/slice.
when_to_use: Lifetime/stable banding (customer tier by total spend, price bands)
avoid_when: The band must react to report filters (use dynamic segmentation)
common_mistakes: Overlapping config ranges -> multiple matches/errors
Implementing as a measure when an axis is needed
patterns.retail_example: Customer[Segment] = VAR s=CALCULATE([Sales Amount],ALLEXCEPT('Customer','Customer'[CustomerKey])) RETURN CALCULATE(VALUES('Customer Segments'[Segment]), FILTER('Customer Segments','Customer Segments'[Min Sales]<=s && 'Customer Segments'[Max Sales]>s))
future_agent_use: model review: check config table for overlapping/gapped ranges
generator: scaffold band column from a config table

patterns.purpose: Classify entities by a measure value at query time, honoring report filters.
patterns.dax_shape: SUMX(VALUES(grainCol), VAR set=FILTER(VALUES(entityKey), [m] in [SegMin,SegMax]) RETURN CALCULATE(<agg>, KEEPFILTERS(set)))
patterns.key_rule: Band depends on a measure -> measure-based; use KEEPFILTERS to intersect the computed set with user filters, and iterate the grain to keep totals correct.
when_to_use: Customer tier by spend in the filtered period
avoid_when: A stable, filter-independent band is wanted (use static)
common_mistakes: Omitting KEEPFILTERS -> set replaces user selection
Not iterating grain -> wrong non-additive total
Overlapping config ranges
patterns.retail_example: Customers in Segment := SUMX(VALUES('Date'[Year]), VAR lo=MIN('Customer Segments'[Min Sales]) VAR hi=MAX('Customer Segments'[Max Sales]) VAR set=FILTER(VALUES('Customer'[CustomerKey]), VAR cs=[Sales Amount] RETURN cs>lo && cs<=hi) RETURN CALCULATE(DISTINCTCOUNT('Sales'[CustomerKey]), KEEPFILTERS(set)))
future_agent_use: analyzer: flag missing KEEPFILTERS in set-filter measures
analyzer: detect non-additive totals not aggregated at grain

patterns.purpose: Group entities into A/B/C by cumulative contribution to a total (70/20/10).
patterns.dax_shape: Per entity: cumPct = cumulative(value desc)/grandTotal ; SWITCH(TRUE(), cumPct<=.7,'A', cumPct<=.9,'B','C')
patterns.key_rule: Static ABC = calculated column (stable axis); dynamic ABC = measures (reacts to filters, slower); per-period = snapshot table.
avoid_when: Thresholds change constantly and must be filter-driven at fine grain without snapshots (watch performance)
common_mistakes: Computing cumulative on the wrong sort direction
Using a calc column when per-year classes are needed (use snapshot)
patterns.retail_example: Product[ABC Class] = VAR ps=CALCULATE([Sales Amount],ALLEXCEPT('Product','Product'[ProductKey])) VAR tot=CALCULATE([Sales Amount],ALL('Product')) VAR cum=CALCULATE([Sales Amount],ALL('Product'),FILTER(ALL('Product'),CALCULATE([Sales Amount],ALLEXCEPT('Product','Product'[ProductKey]))>=ps)) RETURN SWITCH(TRUE(),DIVIDE(cum,tot)<=.7,"A",DIVIDE(cum,tot)<=.9,"B","C")
future_agent_use: generator: ABC column or snapshot table from a value measure + thresholds
model review: recommend snapshot when per-period classes requested

patterns.purpose: Count new / returning / lost / recovered customers within a period.
patterns.dax_shape: new = customers whose global MIN(OrderDate) falls inside the visible period (compare first-ever purchase vs period start)
patterns.key_rule: Define new/returning/lost precisely FIRST (and whether other filters apply) - it changes both formula and performance.
avoid_when: Using the most-flexible variant on large data when a simpler definition suffices (it is expensive)
common_mistakes: Letting category/store filters silently make a customer 'new' multiple times
Iterating measures over the whole fact (slow)
patterns.retail_example: New Customers := VAR cust=VALUES('Sales'[CustomerKey]) VAR start=MIN('Date'[Date]) RETURN COUNTROWS(FILTER(cust, VAR first=CALCULATE(MIN('Sales'[OrderDate]),ALLEXCEPT('Sales','Sales'[CustomerKey])) RETURN first>=start))
future_agent_use: generator: new/returning/lost/recovered family from a chosen definition
analyzer: warn on costly flexible variant at high cardinality
patterns.source: DAX Patterns ch. New and returning customers

patterns.purpose: Count distinct values of an attribute related through the fact (e.g. distinct categories bought).
patterns.dax_shape: CALCULATE ( DISTINCTCOUNT ( 'Dim'[Attr] ), 'Sales' )  -- or via TREATAS/bridge depending on direction
patterns.key_rule: Distinct counts don't naturally flow from many-side to one-side; route the filter explicitly (cross-filter, summarize, or TREATAS).
avoid_when: A simple DISTINCTCOUNT on the fact key already answers it
common_mistakes: Expecting the one-side to be filtered by the many-side without help
Heavy DISTINCTCOUNT in a hot path without testing alternatives
patterns.retail_example: Distinct Categories Sold := CALCULATE ( DISTINCTCOUNT ( 'Product'[Category] ), 'Sales' )
future_agent_use: analyzer: detect cross-table distinct counts that need explicit propagation
perf: suggest bridge vs TREATAS test
patterns.source: DAX Patterns ch. Related distinct count

patterns.purpose: Count entities active at a reference date (started <= D, not yet ended).
patterns.dax_shape: VAR d=MAX('Date'[Date]) RETURN CALCULATE(<count>, 'F'[Start]<=d && 'F'[End]>d, REMOVEFILTERS('Date'))
patterns.key_rule: Turn the Date axis into a single reference point, clear the Date filter, then express the overlap predicate on start/end columns.
avoid_when: Events have no start/end interval
common_mistakes: Leaving the Date relationship filtering the fact (double-counts/misses)
Using <= on both ends inconsistently
patterns.retail_example: Open Orders := VAR d=MAX('Date'[Date]) RETURN CALCULATE(DISTINCTCOUNT('Sales'[SalesKey]), 'Sales'[OrderDate]<=d && 'Sales'[DeliveryDate]>d, REMOVEFILTERS('Date'))
future_agent_use: generator: active-at-date measures from start/end columns
analyzer: verify Date filter is neutralized
patterns.source: DAX Patterns ch. Events in progress

patterns.purpose: Rank entities by a measure, statically (fixed) or dynamically (reacts to filters), and filter Top-N.
patterns.dax_shape: static col: RANKX(ALL('Dim'),[m],,DESC,DENSE)  |  dynamic measure: IF(HASONEVALUE(key), RANKX(ALLSELECTED(attr),[m],,DESC,DENSE))
patterns.key_rule: Static rank = calculated column over ALL; dynamic rank = measure over ALLSELECTED; always guard the total row.
avoid_when: You actually need Top-N filtering of values (combine with TOPN + KEEPFILTERS)
common_mistakes: Ranking the total row (no HASONEVALUE guard)
Wrong ties mode (DENSE vs SKIP)
ALL vs ALLSELECTED confusion
patterns.retail_example: Product Rank := IF ( HASONEVALUE('Product'[ProductKey]), RANKX ( ALLSELECTED('Product'[Product Name]), [Sales Amount],, DESC, DENSE ) )
future_agent_use: generator: static/dynamic/within-group rank from a base measure
analyzer: missing total-row guard or wrong ALL scope

patterns.purpose: Let users drive logic via a disconnected slicer (Top-N, scenario rate, target).
patterns.dax_shape: VAR p=SELECTEDVALUE('Param'[Value], default) RETURN <expression using p>
patterns.key_rule: The parameter table has NO relationship; read it with SELECTEDVALUE and provide a default for the no/multi-selection case.
avoid_when: The value can come from a real related dimension instead
common_mistakes: No default in SELECTEDVALUE
Relating the parameter table to the model by mistake
Forgetting KEEPFILTERS when applying a dynamic set
patterns.retail_example: Sales Top N Products := VAR n=SELECTEDVALUE('Top N Param'[Top N],10) VAR t=TOPN(n,VALUES('Product'[ProductKey]),[Sales Amount],DESC) RETURN CALCULATE([Sales Amount], KEEPFILTERS(t))
future_agent_use: generator: scaffold disconnected param table + measure
analyzer: flag missing SELECTEDVALUE default

patterns.purpose: Convert amounts to a reporting currency at the correct-grain exchange rate.
patterns.dax_shape: SUMX ( VALUES('Date'[Date]), [Amount] * <daily rate for that date> )
patterns.key_rule: Convert at the transaction-date (or chosen) rate at the right grain, THEN sum; never convert an aggregate at a single rate.
avoid_when: Single-currency model
common_mistakes: Applying one period-end rate to an already-summed amount
Missing rate for a date (no fallback)
Wrong rate direction (multiply vs divide)
patterns.retail_example: Sales (Reporting) := SUMX ( VALUES('Date'[Date]), VAR r=CALCULATE(SELECTEDVALUE('ExchangeRate'[Rate]),'ExchangeRate'[Date]=MAX('Date'[Date])) RETURN [Sales Amount]*r )
future_agent_use: model review: detect multi-currency facts needing conversion
generator: conversion measure with chosen rate policy

patterns.purpose: Time calculations (WTD, prior-week, rolling weeks, YOY by week) on a non-Gregorian / 4-4-5 calendar where built-in TI fails.
patterns.dax_shape: Navigate via sequential period-index columns on a custom Date table: VAR cur=MAX('Date'[Week Index]) ... CALCULATE([m],'Date'[Week Index] IN GENERATESERIES(cur-N+1,cur))
patterns.key_rule: Built-in TI functions only work on a standard Gregorian calendar; for weeks/4-4-5 navigate with your own sequential index columns instead.
when_to_use: Fiscal weeks not aligned to months
avoid_when: A standard Gregorian calendar suffices (use built-in TI)
common_mistakes: Using DATESYTD/SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR on a week calendar
No contiguous week index
Comparing week 53 across years without handling
patterns.retail_example: Sales WTD := VAR wk=MAX('Date'[Year Week]) RETURN CALCULATE([Sales Amount], 'Date'[Year Week]=wk, 'Date'[Date]<=MAX('Date'[Date]))
future_agent_use: generator: week/period measures from index columns
analyzer: flag built-in TI on a custom calendar
model review: verify contiguous week index

patterns.purpose: Flatten a self-referencing parent-child structure (chart of accounts, org chart) into browsable level columns.
patterns.dax_shape: Calc columns: Path=PATH(key,parentKey); Level1=LOOKUPVALUE(name, key, PATHITEM(Path,1,INTEGER)); ... plus depth/IsLeaf helpers
patterns.key_rule: Use PATH/PATHITEM/PATHLENGTH to materialize levels as calculated columns; handle ragged hierarchies by repeating or blanking deeper levels.
avoid_when: A fixed natural hierarchy already exists as columns
common_mistakes: Not handling ragged depths (gaps in the matrix)
Browsing the raw key/parent instead of flattened levels
Unbounded depth
patterns.retail_example:
Account[Path] = PATH('Account'[AccountKey], 'Account'[ParentAccountKey])
Account[Level 1] = LOOKUPVALUE('Account'[Account Name], 'Account'[AccountKey], PATHITEM('Account'[Path], 1, INTEGER))
future_agent_use: generator: scaffold PATH columns + level lookups
model review: detect self-referencing keys needing flattening

patterns.purpose: Count entities that moved from one class in a prior period to another class in the current period (e.g. segment migration).
patterns.dax_shape: Two disconnected class axes (From, To); per entity compute class at each period boundary, then count entities matching the (From,To) cell with KEEPFILTERS
patterns.key_rule: Evaluate each entity's class at the correct period boundaries, then cross-tabulate with disconnected from/to axes applied via TREATAS/KEEPFILTERS.
when_to_use: Customer segment migration month over month
avoid_when: Only a single-period classification is needed (use segmentation)
common_mistakes: Class evaluated in the wrong period context
Overlapping class ranges
Counting rows instead of distinct entities
patterns.retail_example: Customers Moved := VAR fromC=SELECTEDVALUE('Seg From'[Segment]) VAR toC=SELECTEDVALUE('Seg To'[Segment]) RETURN COUNTROWS(FILTER(VALUES('Customer'[CustomerKey]), [Segment Prev Year]=fromC && [Segment This Year]=toC))
future_agent_use: generator: from/to migration measure from a class measure
analyzer: verify class evaluated at right boundaries

patterns.purpose: Analyze survey responses (respondents x questions x answers) with flexible filtering, counting distinct respondents.
patterns.dax_shape: Bridge between Respondents and Answers; measures use DISTINCTCOUNT(respondent) with filters routed across the many-to-many via the bridge or TREATAS
patterns.key_rule: Count distinct respondents (not answer rows), and route the many-to-many filter explicitly through the bridge.
avoid_when: A simple one-to-many answer model suffices
common_mistakes: Counting answer rows instead of distinct respondents
Relying on bidirectional relationships instead of an explicit bridge
Filters not propagating across the m2m
patterns.retail_example:
Respondents := DISTINCTCOUNT('Answers'[RespondentKey])
Respondents for Answer := CALCULATE([Respondents], TREATAS(VALUES('Answer Filter'[AnswerKey]), 'Answers'[AnswerKey]))
future_agent_use: generator: distinct-respondent measures over a bridge
model review: detect m2m needing a bridge

patterns.purpose: Find orders that contain product A and also contain product B (market-basket affinity).
patterns.dax_shape: A second disconnected Product table for the 'and also' role; filter orders containing A, then count orders that also contain the TREATAS-applied B set
patterns.key_rule: Self-relate the fact through a second (disconnected) product table applied with TREATAS; count distinct orders, and exclude A=B.
when_to_use: 'Customers who bought X also bought Y'
avoid_when: Simple co-occurrence within one product dimension is enough
common_mistakes: Counting lines instead of distinct orders
Letting A and B be the same product
Not isolating the second product role
patterns.retail_example: Orders with A and B := VAR ordersWithA=CALCULATETABLE(VALUES('Sales'[OrderKey])) RETURN CALCULATE(DISTINCTCOUNT('Sales'[OrderKey]), ordersWithA, TREATAS(VALUES('Product B'[ProductKey]),'Sales'[ProductKey]))
future_agent_use: generator: affinity measure with a second product table
analyzer: verify distinct-order counting and A!=B

patterns.purpose: Compare actuals (fine grain) to budget (coarse grain) without breaking when filtering by columns budget lacks.
patterns.dax_shape: Relate Budget at its grain (e.g. Month, Category); align via shared higher-grain dims (TREATAS if needed); Variance=Actual-Budget; Variance %=DIVIDE(...)
patterns.key_rule: Filter the budget table only by the dimensions/columns it actually supports; align actuals and budget through shared higher-grain attributes.
when_to_use: Targets set at month/category but sales at day/product
avoid_when: Both facts share the same grain (simple subtraction)
common_mistakes: Filtering budget by a column it doesn't have (returns blank/odd)
Comparing at mismatched grain
Variance % without DIVIDE
patterns.retail_example:
Budget Amount := SUM('Budget'[Budget Amount])
Variance := [Sales Amount] - [Budget Amount]
Variance % := DIVIDE([Variance], [Budget Amount])
future_agent_use: generator: actual/budget/variance family at budget grain
model review: detect grain mismatch & unsupported filters
